session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 35 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 113 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

90   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
92 } migration_state_t;

Parameters

Returns

2273   if (!session) {
2274     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2275     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2276   }
2277 
2278   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2279            session->participant_id[1]);
2280 
2281   // Get configured port (or use default)
2282   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2283 
2284   // Mark ourselves as host
2285   session->is_host = true;
2286 
2287   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2288   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2289     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2290         .port = host_port,
2291         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2292         .max_clients = 32,
2293         .encryption_enabled = true,
2294     };
2295 
2296     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2297     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2298       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2299       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2300     }
2301 
2302     // Start listening for new participant connections
2303     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2304     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2305       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2306       return hstart;
2307     }
2308 
2309     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2310   }
2311 
2312   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2313   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2314   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2315   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2316   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2317   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2318   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2319   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2320   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2321 
2322   if (session->acds_transport) {
2323     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement, sizeof(announcement), 0);
2324     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2325              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2326   }
2327 
2328   // Mark migration complete
2329   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2330 
2331   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2332   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2333 
2334   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2335 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2149 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2149                                                                                    {
2150   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2151     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2152     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2153   }
2154 
2155   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2156   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2157     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2158     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2159       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2160     }
2161     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2162     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2163   }
2164 
2165   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2166   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2167   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2168     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2169   }
2170 
2171   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2172   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2173     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2174   }
2175 
2176   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2177 
2178   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2179   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2180     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2181     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2182       // Ping timed out
2183       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2184       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2185       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2186                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2187 
2188       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2189       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2190         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2191         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2192       }
2193     }
2194   }
2195 
2196   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2197   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2198   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2199     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2200     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2201     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2202       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2203       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2204         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2205         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2206         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2207                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2208       } else {
2209         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2210         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2211         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2212           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2213           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2214         }
2215       }
2216     }
2217   }
2218 
2219   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2220 }

2338   if (!session) {
2339     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2340     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2341   }
2342 
2343   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2344            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2345 
2346   // Destroy old participant context if present
2347   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2348     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2349     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2350   }
2351 
2352   // Create new participant context for the future host
2353   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2354       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2355       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2356       .enable_audio = true,
2357       .enable_video = true,
2358       .encryption_enabled = true,
2359   };
2360 
2361   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2362   if (!session->participant_ctx) {

2364     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2365   }
2366

2370     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2371     return pconn;
2372   }
2373 
2374   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2375 
2376   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2377   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2378   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2379   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2380 
2381   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2382            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2383 
2384   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2385   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2386 
2387   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2388   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2389 
2390   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2391 }

Config Module Session

New session or NULL on error

51                                                                                 {
52   if (!config) {
53     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
54     return NULL;
55   }
56 
57   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
58   if (!session) {
59     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
60     return NULL;
61   }
62 
63   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
64   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
65   session->acds_transport = NULL;
66   session->peer_manager = NULL;
67   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
68   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
69   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
70   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
71   session->stun_servers = NULL;
72   session->stun_count = 0;
73   session->turn_servers = NULL;
74   session->turn_count = 0;
75 
76   // Initialize host liveness detection
77   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
78   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
80   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
81   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
82   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
83   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
84 
85   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
86            session->host_ctx);
87 
88   // Load or generate identity key
89   char identity_path[256];
90   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
91   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
93     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
94       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
95       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
96       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
97       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
98         // Save for future use
99         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
100       }
101     }
102   }
103 
104   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
105     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
106     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
107     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
108   } else {
109     char fingerprint[65];
110     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
111     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
112   }
113 
114   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
115   const options_t *opts = options_get();
116   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
117     // Load all identity keys from options
118     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
119       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
120       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
121       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
122       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
123         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
124         char fingerprint[65];
125         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
126         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
127       } else {
128         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
129       }
130     }
131     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
132     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
133   } else {
134     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
135     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
136     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
137   }
138 
139   // ACDS connection info
140   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
141     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
142   } else {
143     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
144   }
145   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
146 
147   // Session string (if joining)
148   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
149     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
150     session->is_initiator = false;
151   } else {
152     session->is_initiator = true;
153   }
154 
155   // Callbacks
156   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
157   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
158   session->on_error = config->on_error;
159   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
160   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
161   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
162 
163   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
164             session->acds_port);
165 
166   return session;
167 }

171     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

174 
175   // Close ACDS connection
176   if (session->acds_transport) {
177     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
178     session->acds_transport = NULL;
179   }
180   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
181     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
182     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
183   }
184 
185   // Destroy host context if active
186   if (session->host_ctx) {
187     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
188     session->host_ctx = NULL;
189   }
190 
191   // Destroy participant context if active
192   if (session->participant_ctx) {
193     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
194     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
195   }
196 
197   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
198   if (session->peer_manager) {
199     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
200     session->peer_manager = NULL;
201   }
202 
203   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
204   if (session->stun_servers) {
205     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
206     session->stun_servers = NULL;
207     session->stun_count = 0;
208   }
209 
210   if (session->turn_servers) {
211     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
212     session->turn_servers = NULL;
213     session->turn_count = 0;
214   }
215 
216   SAFE_FREE(session);
217 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2222 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2222                                                                                                                      {
2223   if (!session) {
2224     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2225     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2226   }
2227 
2228   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2229 
2230   // Initialize migration context
2231   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2232   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2233   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2234   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2235 
2236   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2237   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2238   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2239   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2240   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2241   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2242   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2243 
2244   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2245 
2246   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2247   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2248     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2249     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2250     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2251     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2252     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2253   }
2254 
2255   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2256   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2257   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2258 
2259   // Check if I am the future host
2260   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2261     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2262     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2263     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2264   } else {
2265     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2266     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2267              session->ring.future_host_port);
2268     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2269   }
2270 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1445 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1445                                                                                               {
1446   if (!session) {
1447     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1448   }
1449 
1450   // Handle state-specific processing
1451   switch (session->state) {
1452   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1453     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1454     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1455       // Set socket timeout
1456       if (socket_set_timeout(session->acds_socket, timeout_ns) == 0) {
1457         packet_type_t type;
1458         void *data = NULL;
1459         size_t len = 0;
1460         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1461 
1462         // Try to receive a packet (non-blocking with timeout)
1463         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1464         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1465           if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1466             log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1467             set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1468             SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1469           } else {
1470             // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1471             log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1472             SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1473           }
1474         } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1475           // Timeout is normal, just return
1476         } else {
1477           log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1478         }
1479       }
1480     } else {
1481       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1482       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1483     }
1484     break;
1485   }
1486 
1487   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1488     // Handle NAT negotiation
1489     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1490       // Initialize negotiation
1491       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1492       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1493 
1494       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1495       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1496       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1497         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1498         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1499       }
1500     }
1501 
1502     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1503     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1504       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1505       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1506         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1507         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1508       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1509         // Real error (not just timeout)
1510         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1511       }
1512     }
1513 
1514     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1515     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1516       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1517       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1518       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1519       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1520         // Store result
1521         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1522 
1523         if (session->is_host) {
1524           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1525           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1526                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1527                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1528           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1529           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1530           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1531           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1532         } else {
1533           // They won - we'll be a participant
1534           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1535                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1536                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1537                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1538           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1539           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1540           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1541         }
1542       } else {
1543         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1544         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1545       }
1546     } else {
1547       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1548       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1549     }
1550     break;
1551   }
1552 
1553   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1554     // Start hosting
1555     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1556       // Create host context with configured port
1557       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1558 
1559       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1560           .port = host_port,
1561           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1562           .max_clients = 32,
1563           .encryption_enabled = true,
1564       };
1565 
1566       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1567       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1568         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1569         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1570         break;
1571       }
1572 
1573       // Start listening
1574       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1575       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1576         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1577         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1578         break;
1579       }
1580 
1581       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1582     }
1583 
1584     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1585     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1586       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1587     }
1588 
1589     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1590     break;
1591   }
1592 
1593   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1594     // Connect to host as participant
1595 
1596     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1597     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1598       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1599       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1600         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1601         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1602         break;
1603       }
1604 
1605       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1606       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1607         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1608                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1609 
1610         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1611         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1612             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1613 
1614         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1615           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1616           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1617           break;
1618         }
1619 
1620         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1621         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1622         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1623       }
1624 
1625       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1626       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1627         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1628         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1629 
1630         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1631           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1632                     timeout_ms);
1633 
1634           // Check if we have retries remaining
1635           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1636           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1637             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1638             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1639 
1640             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1641                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1642 
1643             // Wait for backoff period
1644             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1645 
1646             // Destroy old peer manager
1647             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1648             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1649 
1650             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1651             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1652             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1653               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1654               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1655 
1656               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1657               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1658                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1659                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1660                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1661               }
1662 
1663               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1664             }
1665 
1666             // Reset connection state for retry
1667             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1668             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1669 
1670             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1671                      max_attempts + 1);
1672           } else {
1673             // No more retries - connection failed
1674             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1675 
1676             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1677             char error_msg[256];
1678             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1679                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1680             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1681 
1682             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1683             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1684               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1685               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1686               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1687             }
1688 
1689             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1690           }
1691         }
1692       }
1693 
1694       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1695       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1696       if (session->acds_transport) {
1697         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1698         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1699         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1700         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1701 
1702         fd_set readfds;
1703         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1704         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1705 
1706         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1707         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1708           // Data available to read
1709           packet_type_t type;
1710           void *data = NULL;
1711           size_t len = 0;
1712           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1713 
1714           asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1715 
1716           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1717             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1718             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1719             SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1720           } else {
1721             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1722           }
1723         }
1724       }
1725 
1726       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1727       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1728       break;
1729     }
1730 
1731     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1732       // Create participant context for this session
1733       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1734           .address = session->host_address,
1735           .port = session->host_port,
1736           .enable_audio = true,
1737           .enable_video = true,
1738           .encryption_enabled = true,
1739       };
1740 
1741       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1742       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1743         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1744         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1745         break;
1746       }
1747 
1748       // Attempt connection
1749       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1750       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1751         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1752         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1753         break;
1754       }
1755 
1756       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1757 
1758       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1759       // Get the socket from participant context
1760       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1761       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1762         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1763         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1764         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1765         break;
1766       }
1767 
1768       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1769       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1770       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1771       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1772       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1773       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1774       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1775       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1776       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1777 
1778       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1779       if (result != 0) {
1780         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1781         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1782         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1783         break;
1784       }
1785 
1786       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1787       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1788       packet_type_t packet_type;
1789       void *payload = NULL;
1790       size_t payload_len = 0;
1791 
1792       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1793       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1794         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1795         if (payload) {
1796           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1797         }
1798         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1799         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1800         break;
1801       }
1802 
1803       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1804         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1805         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1806         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1807         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1808         break;
1809       }
1810 
1811       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1812       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1813       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1814 
1815       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1816         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1817         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1818         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1819         break;
1820       }
1821 
1822       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1823                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1824 
1825       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1826       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1827       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1828       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1829       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1830       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1831       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1832       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1833       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1834       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1835 
1836       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1837       if (result != 0) {
1838         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1839         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1840         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1841         break;
1842       }
1843 
1844       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1845       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1846     }
1847 
1848     // Check if connection is established
1849     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1850     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1851     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1852               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1853 
1854     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1855       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1856       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1857     }
1858 
1859     if (is_connected) {
1860       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1861       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1862     } else {
1863       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1864                 "is_connected=%d)",
1865                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1866     }
1867     break;
1868   }
1869 
1870   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1871     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1872     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
1873       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1874       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1875 
1876       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1877 
1878       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1879         // Data available to read
1880         packet_type_t type;
1881         void *data = NULL;
1882         size_t len = 0;
1883         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1884 
1885         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1886 
1887         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1888           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1889           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1890           SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1891         }
1892       }
1893     }
1894 
1895     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1896     if (!session->is_host) {
1897       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1898       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1899       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1900         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1901         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1902 
1903         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1904         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1905         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1906           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1907           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1908           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1909           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1910         }
1911 
1912         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1913         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1914         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1915       }
1916     }
1917     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1918     break;
1919 
1920   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1921     // Host migration in progress
1922     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1923     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1924       // Migration completed successfully
1925       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1926       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1927     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1928       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1929       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1930       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1931       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1932     }
1933     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1934     break;
1935 
1936   default:
1937     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
1938     break;
1939   }
1940 
1941   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1942 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1296 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1296                                                                         {
1297   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1298 
1299   if (!session) {
1300     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1301   }
1302 
1303   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1304            session->session_string);
1305 
1306   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1307   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1308     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1309     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1310   }
1311 
1312   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1313   // Connect to ACDS
1314   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1315   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1316     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1317     return result;
1318   }
1319   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1320 
1321   // Check again after connection
1322   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1323     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1324     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1325   }
1326 
1327   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1328   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1329   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1330   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1331 
1332   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1333   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1334     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1335     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1336     return result;
1337   }
1338 
1339   // Store identity key in handshake context
1340   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1341   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1342   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1343   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1344 
1345   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1346   {
1347     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1348     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1349     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1350     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1351 
1352     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1353     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1354       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1355       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1356       return result;
1357     }
1358 
1359     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1360     SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1361 
1362     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1363       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1364       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1365       return result;
1366     }
1367   }
1368 
1369   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1370   {
1371     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1372     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1373     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1374     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1375 
1376     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1377     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1378       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1379       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1380       return result;
1381     }
1382 
1383     result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1384     SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1385 
1386     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1387       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1388       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1389       return result;
1390     }
1391   }
1392 
1393   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1394   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1395   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1396   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1397     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1398     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1399     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1400     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1401 
1402     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1403     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1404       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1405       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1406       return result;
1407     }
1408 
1409     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1410     SAFE_FREE(alloc_buffer);
1411 
1412     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1413       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1414       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1415       return result;
1416     }
1417   } else {
1418     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1419   }
1420 
1421   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1422 
1423   // Check again after handshake
1424   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1425     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1426     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1427   }
1428 
1429   // Create or join session
1430   if (session->is_initiator) {
1431     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1432     result = create_session(session);
1433     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1434              session->participant_ctx, result);
1435     return result;
1436   } else {
1437     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1438     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);

  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)

Parameters


Session Session context


Definition at line 1944 of file

1944                                                           {
1945   if (!session) {
1946     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1947     return;
1948   }
1949 
1950   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1951 
1952   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1953   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1954     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1955     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1956     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1957     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1958 
1959     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg), 0);
1960   }
1961 
1962   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
1963 }
